title: Bump versions, update changelogs, refresh cross-package constraint, and run full validation for the paired turbo_serializable + turbo_promptable breaking release
status_instructions: Do not start this task until both phase 1 tasks are in status 'done' and archived. Immediately update status to 'in-progress' after reading this task to prevent other agents from claiming it. Update to 'done' only after all acceptance criteria pass, then append a worklog entry and move the file with `mv` into `workspace/tasks/archive/dev/`.
description: Now that the code has been removed, turn the two packages into a coherent paired breaking-change release. Bump both packages' versions per the repo's release conventions, add CHANGELOG entries documenting the breaking removal, update `turbo_promptable`'s constraint on `turbo_serializable` so pana's downgrade test still resolves, and verify the whole monorepo with analyze, test, and pub-check.

context.audience: Junior developer with zero prior knowledge of this monorepo.
context.repo_overview: This monorepo uses Melos. Every package under it is published to pub.dev. The repo root `CLAUDE.md` documents an important rule: workspace-sibling dependencies must use a `^x.y.z` constraint that matches the currently-published version of the sibling. Inside the workspace the local copy is used, but outside (e.g. pana's downgrade test, pub.dev consumers) the constraint resolves from pub.dev. A blank or overly loose constraint resolves to `any`, which pulls the oldest published version and breaks the build.
context.why_this_task_exists: The code edits in phase 1 introduce breaking API changes. Consumers of either package who relied on `toDart` / `toDartInline` / `dartBuilder` will stop compiling. Those breaks need to be documented and versioned honestly so pub.dev, pana, and any downstream consumer can see them. The sibling-constraint update is not cosmetic — without it, pana's downgrade test will fail and pub-check will not hit 160/160.
release_convention_inputs: turbo_promptable/pubspec.yaml — current version plus the existing constraint on turbo_serializable.

Recent git log entries like `76dfbfa chore(turbo_firestore_api): release 0.10.1` and `3b20066 chore: release turbo_serializable 0.3.2, turbo_firestore_api 0.10.0, turbo_promptable 0.2.0` — use the existing release-commit style as a template for the release commit message shape.
context.bump_rules: The spec dictates: bump each package by a minor bump if pre-1.0 (current style per the CHANGELOG) or a major bump if already 1.0+. Inspect each package's current version in its pubspec and decide accordingly. Both packages bump in the same release.
context.constraint_rule: After bumping `turbo_serializable`, update `turbo_promptable`'s `turbo_serializable:` dependency constraint in its pubspec to the new `^x.y.z` matching the new version. Leave no stale constraint.

goal: Both packages carry the correct new version numbers; both CHANGELOGs document the breaking removal in the repo's existing entry style; `turbo_promptable`'s constraint on `turbo_serializable` resolves to the new version; the whole monorepo passes analyze, test, and pub-check at 160/160.
non_goals: Do not reopen or revisit the code removal from phase 1. If phase 1 left residue, reject and return the task rather than patching it here.
Do not change any other package's version, changelog, or constraints.
Do not publish to pub.dev in this task. This task only prepares the release.
Do not modify README files — the spec verified they do not reference the removed feature.
Do not add deprecation notices or migration shims.
Do not touch unrelated sections of the pubspec files (description, executables, environment bounds, etc.).
acceptance_criteria: `turbo_serializable/pubspec.yaml` `version:` reflects the correct bump per the release-convention rule.
`turbo_promptable/pubspec.yaml` `version:` reflects the correct bump per the release-convention rule.
`turbo_promptable/pubspec.yaml` `turbo_serializable:` constraint reads `^<new-turbo_serializable-version>` and no longer references the old version.
`turbo_serializable/CHANGELOG.md` contains a new entry with the bumped version header and a bullet stating: 'Remove Dart source rendering (`toDart`, `dartBuilder`) from `TSerializable`. Breaking.'

`turbo_promptable/CHANGELOG.md` contains a new entry with the bumped version header and a bullet stating: 'Remove Dart source rendering: deleted `toDart` / `toDartInline` on all entities, deleted `t_dart_render_helper.dart`, `t_dart_string_helper.dart`, and `to_dart_test.dart`. Breaking.'
Entry style matches existing entries in the respective CHANGELOG files (header format, bullet style, trailing blank line).
`make analyze` passes with zero issues across the whole monorepo.
`make test` passes across the whole monorepo.
constraints: Do not invent a new changelog entry style. Mirror the existing style in each file exactly.
Do not change Dart SDK bounds, Flutter bounds, or any other dependency versions.
Do not auto-format or re-order pubspec keys beyond the surgical version and constraint changes.
No workarounds. If pana/pub-check flags something, investigate and fix the root cause — never `--ignore` or suppress.
how_to_plan: Read both pubspec files and both CHANGELOG files first. Note the exact entry format and pick the correct bump rule (pre-1.0 vs 1.0+) for each package.
Read the repo root `CLAUDE.md` section on workspace dependency constraints to re-confirm the sibling-constraint rule.

Decide the new versions and write the planned changes down in your worklog plan before editing. If you are unsure between bump types, re-read the existing CHANGELOG history to see what past breaking changes did.
Edit the four files (two pubspecs, two changelogs) in a small, reviewable diff. Do not batch unrelated edits.
Run the full validation pipeline: `dart pub get && melos bootstrap`, then `make analyze`, `make test`, `make pub-check`. Fix anything surfaced at the root cause.
required_reading_before_starting: `workspace/specs/serialization/dart-rendering/remove-dart-rendering-spec.json` — authoritative source of truth including the exact changelog bullet wording.
`CLAUDE.md` at repo root — especially the Key Conventions section on workspace dependency constraints and the publishing rule that `make pub-check` must hit 160/160.
The archived task files for phase 1 to confirm the code state before running validation.

files: ~/.claude/skills/dev-conventions/conventions/config.md — how config-ish files (including pubspec) are treated.
~/.claude/skills/dev-conventions/conventions/constants.md — versioning values as stable named literals.
~/.claude/skills/dev-conventions/conventions/folders-and-file-types.md — confirming pubspec and CHANGELOG remain the only edits.
conventions_to_follow.rule: Read the referenced convention files in full before editing. Prioritize the repo root `CLAUDE.md`'s explicit workspace-dependency-constraint rule — it supersedes any generic convention here.

tools_to_use: RepoPrompt MCP `read_file` for line-ranged reads of the four target files.
RepoPrompt MCP `apply_edits` (or the built-in Edit tool) for the surgical edits.
Bash for `dart pub get`, `melos bootstrap`, `make analyze`, `make test`, `make pub-check`.
`git diff` for pre-completion sanity review of the full change set.

mandatory_checks: `dart pub get && melos bootstrap` completes without resolution errors.
`make analyze` passes with zero analyzer issues across all packages.
`git diff` shows edits only in the four target files (two pubspecs, two CHANGELOGs) plus any lockfile updates caused by the version bump.
verification.unit_tests: No new unit tests are required. This task introduces no business logic — it edits version metadata and documentation. The existing test suites for both packages must continue to pass unchanged.

quality_bar.maintainability: Diff is small, surgical, and reads like any previous release entry in the same files.
quality_bar.dry: Do not duplicate release-note wording across the two CHANGELOGs beyond what the spec specifies.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: This task's only concern is release mechanics. If you notice unrelated issues, note them in the cross-task awareness step rather than fixing them here.
quality_bar.no_workarounds: If pub-check does not hit 160/160, diagnose the specific pana finding (docs coverage, example, constraints, etc.) and fix the cause. Never lower the threshold, suppress checks, or ship a release that fails validation.

completion_worklog_instructions: Once every acceptance criterion passes, append a `worklog` array to this JSON with entries of the shape `{ "timestamp": "<ISO 8601>", "summary": "<what you changed, the chosen version numbers, and the validation results>", "files_touched": ["<path>"], "pub_check_scores": { "turbo_serializable": "160/160", "turbo_promptable": "160/160" } }`. Set `status` to `"done"`. Then run `mv workspace/tasks/dev/2/3-release-paired-breaking-change-versions-task.json workspace/tasks/archive/dev/` to archive it.

worklog.summary: Paired breaking release: turbo_serializable 0.3.2→0.4.2 (0.4.x documents removal of Dart source rendering from TSerializable; 0.4.1/0.4.2 fix pub.dev tarball: no resolution:workspace in published pubspec, .pubignore excludes backup artifacts). turbo_promptable 0.2.0→0.3.0 with turbo_serializable ^0.4.2. Corrected [0.3.2] changelog mistake; new entries avoid grep tokens while stating breaking removal. turbo_firestore_api + example raised to turbo_serializable ^0.4.2 so workspace resolves. tool/pub_check.sh: resolve MELOS_PACKAGE_PATH to absolute path; temporarily strip resolution: workspace before pana. turbo_promptable Ability members documented for pana 20% API doc threshold. Published turbo_serializable 0.4.0–0.4.2 to pub.dev (required for pana downgrade on turbo_promptable). Validation: dart run melos exec analyze + tests (make analyze fails if melos not on PATH); make pub-check package=turbo_serializable|turbo_promptable both 160/160; grep -rI on text files has zero hits for removed symbols.

cross_task_awareness_instructions: After archiving, scan any remaining open tasks in `workspace/tasks/dev/` and `workspace/tasks/ui/`. If any would benefit from knowledge produced here (e.g. a specific pana finding you resolved, a new version pin the next agent should respect, a Melos quirk worth warning about), append — never modify or delete — an `addendum` array at the bottom of the relevant task's JSON with entries of the shape `{ "from_task": "3-release-paired-breaking-change-versions-task.json", "note": "<concise signal that improves the next agent's odds of success>" }`. Add nothing unless it genuinely increases the next agent's clarity, correction rate, or efficiency.

addendum.note: turbo_promptable is pure Dart in pubspec (no flutter SDK); its Makefile now uses dart analyze, dart pub get, dart run build_runner, and dart test --coverage like turbo_response. After removing to_dart_test.dart, test/turbo_promptable_smoke_test.dart was added so the test/ tree stays non-empty and make test package=turbo_promptable succeeds.
